name: Aarakocra
description:
Aarakocra are avian humanoids with feathered wings and taloned feet, native to the upper reaches of the sky. Their bodies are lightweight and built for flight, with a keen awareness of wind and weather honed across generations of life among the clouds.

**Flight.** Because of your wings, you have a Fly Speed equal to your walking speed. You can't use this flying speed if you're wearing medium or heavy armor.

**Talons.** You have talons that you can use to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier slashing damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

**Wind Caller.** Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the {spell:Gust of Wind} spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast the spell with this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of 2nd level or higher. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this species).

properties.text: Flight: Fly Speed equal to walking speed. Can't fly in medium or heavy armor.
Talons: Unarmed strike deals 1d6 + Strength modifier slashing damage.

name: Aasimar
description:
Aasimar are mortals touched by celestial power, born with a spark of divine energy in their souls. They appear largely like their mortal kin, though many have features hinting at their celestial lineage — skin that seems to glow faintly, silver or gold hair, or eyes like pools of light.

**Celestial Resistance.** You have {rule:Resistance} to Necrotic damage and Radiant damage.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

**Healing Hands.** As a Magic action, you touch a creature and roll a number of d4s equal to your Proficiency Bonus. The creature regains a number of Hit Points equal to the total rolled. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

**Light Bearer.** You know the {spell:Light} cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

**Celestial Revelation.** When you reach character level 3, you can transform as a Bonus Action using one of the options below (choose the option each time you transform). The transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it (no action required). Once you transform, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. Once on each of your turns before the transformation ends, you can deal extra damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your Proficiency Bonus, and the extra damage's type is either Necrotic for Necrotic Shroud or Radiant for Heavenly Wings and Inner Radiance.

- **Heavenly Wings.** Two spectral wings sprout from your back temporarily. Until the transformation ends, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed.
- **Inner Radiance.** Searing light temporarily radiates from your eyes and mouth. For the duration, you shed Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet, and at the end of each of your turns, each creature within 10 feet of you takes Radiant damage equal to your Proficiency Bonus.
- **Necrotic Shroud.** Your eyes briefly become pools of darkness, and flightless wings sprout from your back temporarily. Creatures other than your allies within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or have the {condition:Frightened} condition until the end of your next turn.

properties.text: Healing Hands: As a Magic action, touch a creature and roll a number of d4s equal to your Proficiency Bonus. The creature regains that many Hit Points. Usable once per Long Rest.

Celestial Revelation (level 3+): As a Bonus Action, transform for 1 minute (once per Long Rest). Choose Heavenly Wings (Fly Speed equal to Speed), Inner Radiance (shed light; deal Radiant damage equal to Proficiency Bonus to creatures within 10 ft at end of your turn), or Necrotic Shroud (nearby creatures make Charisma save or gain Frightened condition). Each form deals extra damage equal to Proficiency Bonus once per turn.

options.label: Heavenly Wings
options.description: Spectral wings grant a Fly Speed equal to your Speed for 1 minute.

options.label: Inner Radiance
options.description: Shed Bright Light (10 ft) and Dim Light (10 ft); deal Radiant damage equal to Proficiency Bonus to creatures within 10 ft at end of each of your turns.

options.label: Necrotic Shroud
options.description: Creatures within 10 ft (not allies) must succeed on a Charisma save (DC 8 + Charisma modifier + Proficiency Bonus) or be Frightened until end of your next turn.

name: Bugbear
description:
Bugbears are hulking, hairy goblinoids who haunt the wilderness and dark places. Despite their fearsome reputation as ambush predators, bugbears are a people of surprising agility and cunning, with long arms and a talent for moving unseen through tight spaces.

**Creature Type.** You are a Humanoid. You are also considered a goblinoid for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be a goblinoid.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

**Fey Ancestry.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} condition on yourself.

**Long-Limbed.** When you make a melee attack on your turn, your reach for it is 5 feet greater than normal.

**Powerful Build.** You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

**Sneaky.** You are proficient in the Stealth skill. In addition, without squeezing, you can move through and stop in a space large enough for a Small creature.

**Surprise Attack.** If you hit a creature with an attack roll, the creature takes an extra 2d6 damage if it hasn't taken a turn yet in the current combat.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Charmed condition

properties.text: Long-Limbed: Reach for melee attacks on your turn is 5 feet greater than normal.
Powerful Build: Count as one size larger for carrying capacity and weight you can push, drag, or lift.
Sneaky: Can move through and stop in a space large enough for a Small creature without squeezing.
Surprise Attack: On a hit, deal an extra 2d6 damage if the target hasn't taken a turn yet this combat.

name: Centaur
description:
Centaurs are fey creatures with the upper body of a humanoid and the lower body of a horse. They are proud, swift beings who feel a deep connection to the natural world. Centaurs are found in forests, meadows, and the border regions between civilization and the wild.

**Creature Type.** You are a Fey.

**Charge.** If you move at least 30 feet straight toward a target and then hit it with a melee weapon attack on the same turn, you can immediately follow that attack with a bonus action, making one attack against the target with your hooves.

**Equine Build.** You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push or drag. In addition, any climb that requires hands and feet is especially difficult for you because of your equine legs. When you make such a climb, each foot of movement costs you 4 extra feet instead of the normal 1 extra foot.

**Hooves.** You have hooves that you can use to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier bludgeoning damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

**Natural Affinity.** Your fey connection to nature gives you proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Medicine, Nature, or Survival.

properties.text: Creature Type: Fey.

Charge: If you move at least 30 feet straight toward a target and hit it with a melee weapon attack on the same turn, you can immediately follow that attack with a Bonus Action, making one attack against the target with your hooves.

Equine Build: Count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push or drag. Any climb that requires hands and feet costs you 4 extra feet of movement per foot instead of the normal 1 extra foot.

Hooves: Your hooves are a natural weapon for unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier bludgeoning damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

name: Changeling
description:
Changelings are fey creatures with an innate talent for shapeshifting. They can alter their appearance at will, allowing them to move through society as virtually anyone. Most changelings adopt a 'true form' for day-to-day life, but they are never entirely bound by it.

**Creature Type.** You are a Fey.

**Changeling Instincts.** Thanks to your connection to the fey realm, you gain proficiency with two of the following skills of your choice: Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Performance, or Persuasion.

**Shapechanger.** As an action, you change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height between Medium and Small. You can make yourself appear as a member of another species, though none of your game statistics change. You can't duplicate the appearance of an individual you've never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have. Your clothing and equipment aren't changed by this trait. You stay in the new form until you use an action to revert to your true form or until you die.

properties.text: Creature Type: Fey.

Shapechanger: As an action, change your appearance and voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height between Medium and Small, and make yourself appear as a member of another species, though none of your game statistics change. You can't duplicate the appearance of an individual you've never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs. Your clothing and equipment aren't changed. You stay in the new form until you use an action to revert to your true form or until you die.

name: Deep Gnome
description:
Deep gnomes, also called svirfneblin, are gnomes of the Underdark who have adapted to subterranean life over countless generations. Their skin has taken on the grey and brown hues of stone, and they have developed powerful magical defenses against the dangers of the deep earth.

**Creature Type.** You are a Humanoid. You are also considered a gnome for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be a gnome.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 120 feet.

**Gift of the Svirfneblin.** Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the {spell:Disguise Self} spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the {spell:Nondetection} spell with it, without requiring a material component. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this species).

**Gnomish Magic Resistance.** You have {rule:Advantage} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against spells.

**Svirfneblin Camouflage.** When you make a Dexterity (Stealth) check, you can make the check with {rule:Advantage}. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: Svirfneblin Camouflage: Can make Dexterity (Stealth) checks with advantage. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

name: Dragonborn
description:
Dragonborn look like wingless, bipedal dragons — and early in their history, some Dragonborn served dragons as soldiers, guards, and personal attendants. Since then, Dragonborn have claimed their own destinies.

**Draconic Ancestry.** Your lineage stems from a dragon progenitor. Choose the kind of dragon from the Draconic Ancestors table (Black/Acid, Blue/Lightning, Brass/Fire, Bronze/Lightning, Copper/Acid, Gold/Fire, Green/Poison, Red/Fire, Silver/Cold, White/Cold). Your choice affects your Breath Weapon and Damage Resistance traits as well as your appearance.

**Breath Weapon.** When you take the {action:Attack} action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with an exhalation of magical energy in either a 15-foot Cone or a 30-foot Line that is 5 feet wide (choose the shape each time). Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, a creature takes 1d10 damage of the type determined by your Draconic Ancestry. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. This damage increases by 1d10 when you reach character levels 5 (2d10), 11 (3d10), and 17 (4d10). You can use this Breath Weapon a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

**Damage Resistance.** You have {rule:Resistance} to the damage type determined by your Draconic Ancestry.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

**Draconic Flight.** When you reach character level 5, you can channel draconic magic to give yourself temporary flight. As a Bonus Action, you sprout spectral wings on your back that last for 10 minutes or until you retract the wings (no action required) or have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition. During that time, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

properties.text: Breath Weapon: Replace one attack with a 15-foot Cone or 30-foot Line exhalation. Dexterity save (DC 8 + Con modifier + Proficiency Bonus) or take 1d10 damage (type from ancestry; scales to 4d10 at level 17). Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.
Draconic Flight (level 5+): Bonus Action to sprout spectral wings granting Fly Speed equal to Speed for 10 minutes. Once per Long Rest.

name: Duergar
description:
Duergar, also called gray dwarves, are a subterranean people who have spent millennia in the Underdark. Centuries of psionic oppression by mind flayers have left an indelible mark on their heritage, granting them resistance to mental manipulation. They have adapted to the harsh depths with powerful magic and an iron constitution.

**Creature Type.** You are a Humanoid. You are also considered a dwarf for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be a dwarf.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 120 feet.

**Duergar Magic.** Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the {spell:Enlarge/Reduce} spell on yourself with this trait, without requiring a material component. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the {spell:Invisibility} spell on yourself with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this species).

**Dwarven Resilience.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Poisoned} condition on yourself. You also have {rule:Resistance} to poison damage.

**Psionic Fortitude.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} or {condition:Stunned} condition on yourself.

properties.text: Dwarven Resilience: Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Poisoned condition.
Psionic Fortitude: Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Charmed or Stunned condition.

name: Dwarf
description:
Dwarves are a short, stocky people found in mountains, hills, and underground vaults. They typically have skin tones that range from deep brown to a lighter tan, with hair that can be black, gray, or red. Dwarven names and culture often reflect their connection to stone, metal, and endurance.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 120 feet.

**Dwarven Resilience.** You have {rule:Resistance} to Poison damage. You also have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Poisoned} condition.

**Dwarven Toughness.** Your Hit Point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 again whenever you gain a level.

**Stonecunning.** As a Bonus Action, you gain {rule:Tremorsense} with a range of 60 feet for 10 minutes. You must be on a stone surface or touching a stone surface to use this Tremorsense. The stone can be natural or worked. You can use this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Poisoned condition

properties.text: Stonecunning: As a Bonus Action, gain Tremorsense 60 ft for 10 minutes (must be on stone surface). Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

name: Eladrin
description:
Eladrin are elves closely tied to the Feywild, embodying its ever-shifting seasons. Their moods and magic resonate with the passage of spring, summer, autumn, and winter. Eladrin can step between the mortal world and the Feywild in an instant, a reflection of their fundamentally fey nature.

**Creature Type.** You are a Humanoid. You are also considered an elf for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be an elf.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

**Fey Ancestry.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} condition on yourself.

**Fey Step.** As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. When you reach 3rd level, your Fey Step gains an additional effect based on your season (DC 8 + proficiency bonus + your chosen spellcasting ability modifier): Autumn — up to two creatures within 10 ft must succeed on a Wisdom save or be {condition:Charmed} by you for 1 minute; Winter — one creature within 5 ft must succeed on a Wisdom save or be {condition:Frightened} until end of your next turn; Spring — touch one willing creature within 5 ft and teleport it instead; Summer — each creature of your choice within 5 ft takes fire damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

**Keen Senses.** You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

**Trance.** You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a {rule:Long Rest} in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness. Whenever you finish this trance, you can change your season, and you can gain two proficiencies that you don't have, each one with a weapon or a tool of your choice. You retain them until you finish your next long rest.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Charmed condition

properties.text: Fey Step: Bonus Action to teleport up to 30 ft to an unoccupied space you can see. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest. At level 3, gains a seasonal effect (save DC 8 + prof + chosen ability): Autumn (Charmed 1 min, up to 2 creatures), Winter (Frightened until end of next turn), Spring (teleport willing ally instead), Summer (fire damage equal to prof to nearby creatures).
Trance: No need to sleep; can't be put to sleep by magic. Long Rest in 4 hours. On finishing trance, can change season and gain two temporary weapon or tool proficiencies until next Long Rest.

options.label: Autumn
options.description: Fey Step: up to two creatures within 10 ft must succeed on a Wisdom save (DC 8 + prof + ability) or be Charmed by you for 1 minute.

options.label: Winter
options.description: Fey Step: one creature within 5 ft must succeed on a Wisdom save (DC 8 + prof + ability) or be Frightened until end of your next turn.

options.label: Spring
options.description: Fey Step: touch one willing creature within 5 ft and teleport it instead of you to an unoccupied space within 30 ft.

options.label: Summer
options.description: Fey Step: each creature of your choice within 5 ft takes fire damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

name: Elf
description:
Elves are a magical people of otherworldly grace. They are typically slender, with pointed ears, and possess an innate talent for magic and keen perception. All elves carry a connection to the fey realm that shapes their ancestry.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

**Elven Lineage.** You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a lineage from the options below. You gain the level 1 benefit of that lineage. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn a higher-level spell as shown. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose when you select the lineage).

- **Drow.** The range of your Darkvision increases to 120 feet. You also know the {spell:Dancing Lights} cantrip. At level 3: {spell:Faerie Fire}. At level 5: {spell:Darkness}.
- **High Elf.** You know the {spell:Prestidigitation} cantrip. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace that cantrip with a different cantrip from the Wizard spell list. At level 3: {spell:Detect Magic}. At level 5: {spell:Misty Step}.
- **Wood Elf.** Your Speed increases to 35 feet. You also know the {spell:Druidcraft} cantrip. At level 3: {spell:Longstrider}. At level 5: {spell:Pass without Trace}.

**Fey Ancestry.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} condition.

**Keen Senses.** You have proficiency in the Insight, Perception, or Survival skill.

**Trance.** You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Charmed condition

properties.text: Trance: No need to sleep; can't be put to sleep by magic. Long Rest requires only 4 hours of trancelike meditation.

options.label: Drow
options.description: Darkvision increases to 120 ft. Know Dancing Lights cantrip. At level 3: Faerie Fire (1/Long Rest). At level 5: Darkness (1/Long Rest).

options.label: High Elf
options.description: Know Prestidigitation cantrip (replaceable on Long Rest with any Wizard cantrip). At level 3: Detect Magic (1/Long Rest). At level 5: Misty Step (1/Long Rest).

options.label: Wood Elf
options.description: Speed increases to 35 ft. Know Druidcraft cantrip. At level 3: Longstrider (1/Long Rest). At level 5: Pass without Trace (1/Long Rest).

name: Fairy
description:
Fairies are Small fey creatures with gossamer wings and an innate connection to the magic of the Feywild. Playful and mischievous, they are often found near ancient forests or fey crossings. Their natural magic lets them shrink, grow, and coax plants into action.

**Creature Type.** You are a Fey.

**Fairy Magic.** You know the {spell:Druidcraft} cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the {spell:Faerie Fire} spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the {spell:Enlarge/Reduce} spell with this trait. Once you cast {spell:Faerie Fire} or {spell:Enlarge/Reduce} with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this species).

**Flight.** Because of your wings, you have a Fly Speed equal to your walking speed. You can't use this flying speed if you're wearing medium or heavy armor.

properties.text: Flight: Fly Speed equal to walking speed. Can't fly in medium or heavy armor.

name: Firbolg
description:
Firbolgs are large, reclusive humanoids with ties to the fey and a deep reverence for nature. They are among the most powerful of the giant-kin, yet they prefer to live peacefully and avoid conflict when possible. Their innate magic allows them to speak with animals and vanish from sight.

**Firbolg Magic.** You can cast {spell:Detect Magic} and {spell:Disguise Self} spells with this trait. When you use this version of {spell:Disguise Self}, you can seem up to 3 feet shorter or taller. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this species).

**Hidden Step.** As a bonus action, you can magically turn {condition:Invisible} until the start of your next turn or until you attack, make a damage roll, or force someone to make a saving throw. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

**Powerful Build.** You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

**Speech of Beast and Leaf.** You have the ability to communicate in a limited manner with Beasts, Plants, and vegetation. They can understand the meaning of your words, though you have no special ability to understand them in return. You have {rule:Advantage} on all Charisma checks you make to influence them.

properties.text: Hidden Step: Bonus Action to turn Invisible until start of your next turn or until you attack, deal damage, or force a saving throw. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.
Powerful Build: Count as one size larger for carrying capacity and weight you can push, drag, or lift.
Speech of Beast and Leaf: Beasts, Plants, and vegetation understand you. You have advantage on Charisma checks to influence them.

name: Genasi
description:
Genasi are the descendants of mortals and genies, carrying the elemental power of the Elemental Planes within their blood. Each genasi embodies one of the four elements — air, earth, fire, or water — which shapes their appearance, abilities, and personality. They are typically robust individuals who feel most at home when surrounded by their element.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

**Elemental Subrace.** Choose one of the four Genasi subraces: Air, Earth, Fire, or Water. Each grants different traits reflecting your elemental heritage.

options.label: Air Genasi
options.description: Speed 35 ft. Lightning resistance. Know Shocking Grasp cantrip. At level 3: Feather Fall (1/Long Rest). At level 5: Levitate (1/Long Rest). Can hold breath indefinitely while not Incapacitated.

properties.text: Unending Breath: Can hold breath indefinitely while not Incapacitated.

options.label: Earth Genasi
options.description: Know Blade Ward cantrip (can also cast as Bonus Action Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest). At level 5: Pass without Trace (1/Long Rest). Can move across difficult terrain without extra movement if using walking speed on ground or floor.

properties.text: Earth Walk: Move across difficult terrain without expending extra movement while using walking speed on the ground or a floor.
Merge with Stone: Blade Ward cantrip can also be cast as a Bonus Action Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

options.label: Fire Genasi
options.description: Fire resistance. Know Produce Flame cantrip. At level 3: Burning Hands (1/Long Rest). At level 5: Flame Blade (1/Long Rest).

options.label: Water Genasi
options.description: Swim speed equal to walking speed. Acid resistance. Amphibious (breathe air and water). Know Acid Splash cantrip. At level 3: Create or Destroy Water (1/Long Rest). At level 5: Water Walk (1/Long Rest).

properties.text: Amphibious: You breathe air and water.
Swim Speed equal to walking speed.

name: Githyanki
description:
Githyanki are a militant, psionic people who dwell on the Astral Plane aboard spelljamming vessels and in the fortress-city of Tu'narath. Centuries ago they were enslaved by mind flayers; since their liberation, the githyanki have cultivated a warrior culture of fierce independence and territorial aggression.

**Astral Knowledge.** Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you gain proficiency in one skill of your choice and with one weapon or tool of your choice, selected from the Player's Handbook, as you momentarily project your consciousness into the Astral Plane. These proficiencies last until the end of your next long rest.

**Githyanki Psionics.** You know the {spell:Mage Hand} cantrip, and the hand is {condition:Invisible} when you cast the cantrip with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the {spell:Jump} spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast {spell:Misty Step} with it. Once you cast {spell:Jump} or {spell:Misty Step} with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. None of these spells require spell components when you cast them with this trait. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability (choose when you select this species).

**Psychic Resilience.** You have {rule:Resistance} to psychic damage.

properties.text: Mage Hand from Githyanki Psionics: the spectral hand is Invisible.
Astral Knowledge: On finishing a Long Rest, gain proficiency in one skill and one weapon or tool of your choice until your next Long Rest.

name: Githzerai
description:
Githzerai are a psionic people who inhabit the tumultuous plane of Limbo, dwelling in monasteries of perfect stillness carved from chaos. Their centuries of mental training following liberation from mind flayer slavery have made them masters of discipline and psychic defense, and many githzerai pursue careers as monks or warriors of the mind.

**Githzerai Psionics.** You know the {spell:Mage Hand} cantrip, and the hand is {condition:Invisible} when you cast the cantrip with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the {spell:Shield} spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the {spell:Detect Thoughts} spell with it. Once you cast {spell:Shield} or {spell:Detect Thoughts} with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. None of these spells require spell components when you cast them with this trait. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability (choose when you select this species).

**Mental Discipline.** Your innate psychic defenses grant you {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} and {condition:Frightened} conditions on yourself.

**Psychic Resilience.** You have {rule:Resistance} to psychic damage.

properties.text: Mental Discipline: Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Charmed or Frightened condition.
Mage Hand from Githzerai Psionics: the spectral hand is Invisible.

name: Gnome
description:
Gnomes are small folk who delight in invention, discovery, and practical jokes. They are among the most intellectually curious of all the peoples, and their connection to magic runs deep. Gnomes typically stand 3 to 4 feet tall and are slender with pointed features.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

**Gnomish Cunning.** You have {rule:Advantage} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

**Gnomish Lineage.** You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose one of the following options; Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait:

- **Forest Gnome.** You know the {spell:Minor Illusion} cantrip. You also always have the {spell:Speak with Animals} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. You can also use any spell slots you have to cast the spell.
- **Rock Gnome.** You know the {spell:Mending} and {spell:Prestidigitation} cantrips. In addition, you can spend 10 minutes casting Prestidigitation to create a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 HP) that produces one chosen Prestidigitation effect when activated as a Bonus Action. You can have three such devices at a time; each falls apart 8 hours after creation or when you dismantle it.

options.label: Forest Gnome
options.description: Know Minor Illusion cantrip. Speak with Animals always prepared; cast without spell slot Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

options.label: Rock Gnome
options.description: Know Mending and Prestidigitation cantrips. Can spend 10 minutes to create Tiny clockwork devices (up to 3 at a time) that produce a Prestidigitation effect on activation.

properties.text: Rock Gnome Tinker: Spend 10 minutes to create a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 HP) that produces one Prestidigitation effect when activated. Maximum 3 devices at a time; each lasts 8 hours.

name: Goblin
description:
Goblins are Small goblinoids who have survived for millennia through cunning, speed, and sheer tenacity. Despite their reputation as pests and brigands, individual goblins can be resourceful, loyal, and fierce. Their nimble reflexes and capacity for sudden bursts of vicious energy make them formidable in a fight.

**Creature Type.** You are a Humanoid. You are also considered a goblinoid for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be a goblinoid.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

**Fey Ancestry.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} condition on yourself.

**Fury of the Small.** When you damage a creature with an attack or a spell and the creature's size is larger than yours, you can cause the attack or spell to deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, and you can use it no more than once per turn.

**Nimble Escape.** You can take the {action:Disengage} or {action:Hide} action as a bonus action on each of your turns.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Charmed condition

properties.text: Fury of the Small: When you damage a larger creature, deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest, once per turn.
Nimble Escape: Can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of your turns.

name: Goliath
description:
Goliaths are massive beings born in the mountains, whose distant ancestors were Giants. They stand 7 to 8 feet tall and are incredibly powerful. Goliaths carry a proud warrior culture shaped by the harshness of alpine life.

**Giant Ancestry.** You are descended from Giants. Choose one of the following benefits — a supernatural boon from your ancestry; you can use the chosen benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest:

- **Cloud's Jaunt (Cloud Giant).** As a Bonus Action, you magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.
- **Fire's Burn (Fire Giant).** When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can also deal 1d10 Fire damage to that target.
- **Frost's Chill (Frost Giant).** When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can also deal 1d6 Cold damage to that target and reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
- **Hill's Tumble (Hill Giant).** When you hit a Large or smaller creature with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can give that target the {condition:Prone} condition.
- **Stone's Endurance (Stone Giant).** When you take damage, you can take a Reaction to roll 1d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled and reduce the damage by that total.
- **Storm's Thunder (Storm Giant).** When you take damage from a creature within 60 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to deal 1d8 Thunder damage to that creature.

**Large Form.** Starting at character level 5, you can change your size to Large as a Bonus Action if you're in a big enough space. This transformation lasts for 10 minutes or until you end it (no action required). For that duration, you have {rule:Advantage} on Strength checks, and your Speed increases by 10 feet. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

**Powerful Build.** You have {rule:Advantage} on any ability check you make to end the {condition:Grappled} condition. You also count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity.

properties.text: Powerful Build: Count as one size larger for carrying capacity.
Large Form (level 5+): Bonus Action to become Large for 10 minutes (once per Long Rest). Gain advantage on Strength checks and +10 ft Speed.

options.label: Cloud's Jaunt (Cloud Giant)
options.description: As a Bonus Action, teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

options.label: Fire's Burn (Fire Giant)
options.description: When you hit with an attack, deal an extra 1d10 Fire damage. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

options.label: Frost's Chill (Frost Giant)
options.description: When you hit with an attack, deal an extra 1d6 Cold damage and reduce target's Speed by 10 ft until your next turn. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

options.label: Hill's Tumble (Hill Giant)
options.description: When you hit a Large or smaller creature with an attack, give it the Prone condition. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

options.label: Stone's Endurance (Stone Giant)
options.description: Reaction when taking damage: roll 1d12 + Constitution modifier and reduce damage by that total. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

options.label: Storm's Thunder (Storm Giant)
options.description: Reaction when a creature within 60 ft deals damage to you: deal 1d8 Thunder damage to that creature. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

name: Halfling
description:
Halflings are small, nimble folk with hardy constitutions and a remarkable knack for getting out of trouble. They stand about 2 to 3 feet tall and tend toward a cheerful, practical outlook on life. Halflings are found throughout the world and fit into most societies with ease.

**Brave.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Frightened} condition.

**Halfling Nimbleness.** You can move through the space of any creature that is a size larger than you, but you can't stop in the same space.

**Luck.** When you roll a 1 on the d20 of a D20 Test, you can reroll the die, and you must use the new roll.

**Naturally Stealthy.** You can take the {action:Hide} action even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Frightened condition

properties.text: Luck: When you roll a 1 on a D20 Test, you can reroll and must use the new result.
Halfling Nimbleness: Can move through spaces of creatures one size larger, but can't stop there.
Naturally Stealthy: Can take the Hide action even when obscured only by a creature at least one size larger than you.

name: Harengon
description:
Harengons are bipedal, humanoid rabbitfolk with long ears, powerful legs, and keen senses. They originated in the Feywild and carry its unpredictability with them.

**Hare-Trigger.** You can add your Proficiency Bonus to your initiative rolls.

**Leporine Senses.** You have proficiency in the {skill:Perception} skill.

**Lucky Footwork.** When you fail a Dexterity saving throw, you can use your reaction to roll a d4 and add it to the save, potentially turning the failure into a success. You can't use this reaction if you're {condition:Prone} or your speed is 0.

**Rabbit Hop.** As a bonus action, you can jump a number of feet equal to five times your Proficiency Bonus, without provoking opportunity attacks. You can use this trait only if your speed is greater than 0. You can use it a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

properties.text: Lucky Footwork: When you fail a Dexterity saving throw, you can use your reaction to roll a d4 and add it to the save. Can't be used if Prone or speed is 0.
Rabbit Hop: As a bonus action, jump up to 5 × Proficiency Bonus feet without provoking opportunity attacks (requires speed > 0). Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

name: Hobgoblin
description:
Hobgoblins are goblinoids with a strong martial tradition rooted in fey magic and bonds of reciprocity. They stand around 5–6 feet tall and are known for their discipline and gift for hospitality.

**Darkvision.** You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

**Fey Ancestry.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} condition on yourself.

**Fey Gift.** You can use this trait to take the {action:Help} action as a bonus action, and you can do so a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Starting at 3rd level, choose one of the options below each time you take the Help action with this trait:

- **Hospitality.** You and the creature you help each gain a number of temporary hit points equal to 1d6 plus your Proficiency Bonus.
- **Passage.** You and the creature you help each increase your walking speeds by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
- **Spite.** Until the start of your next turn, the first time the creature you help hits a target with an attack roll, that target has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes within the next minute.

**Fortune from the Many.** If you miss with an attack roll or fail an ability check or a saving throw, you can draw on your bonds of reciprocity to gain a bonus to the roll equal to the number of allies you can see within 30 feet of you (maximum bonus of +3). You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Charmed condition on yourself

properties.text: Fey Gift: Take the Help action as a bonus action. Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest. At level 3+, choose Hospitality (both gain 1d6 + Proficiency Bonus temp HP), Passage (both gain +10 ft speed until start of next turn), or Spite (target has disadvantage on next attack roll within 1 minute).
Fortune from the Many: When you miss an attack roll or fail a check or save, gain a bonus equal to the number of allies you can see within 30 feet (max +3). Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

name: Human
description:
Humans are the most adaptable and widespread of all the peoples, found in virtually every corner of the world. They are highly variable in appearance, culture, and outlook — it is their flexibility and ambition that defines them rather than any single trait.

**Resourceful.** You gain {rule:Heroic Inspiration} whenever you finish a Long Rest.

**Skillful.** You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.

**Versatile.** You gain an Origin feat of your choice.

properties.text: Resourceful: You gain Heroic Inspiration whenever you finish a Long Rest.
Versatile: You gain one Origin feat of your choice. (Handled at character creation.)

name: Kenku
description:
Kenku are raven-headed humanoids who dwell in cities, lurking at the fringes of human society. They have no voices of their own but possess an uncanny ability to mimic sounds and extraordinary memory.

**Expert Duplication.** When you copy writing or craftwork produced by yourself or someone else, you have {rule:Advantage} on any ability checks you make to produce an exact duplicate.

**Kenku Recall.** Thanks to your supernaturally good memory, you have proficiency in two skills of your choice. Moreover, when you make an ability check using any skill in which you have proficiency, you can give yourself advantage on the check before rolling the d20. You can give yourself advantage in this way a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

**Mimicry.** You can accurately mimic sounds you have heard, including voices. A creature that hears the sounds you make can tell they are imitations only with a successful Wisdom (Insight) check against a DC of 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Charisma modifier.

properties.condition: to produce an exact copy of writing or craftwork (Expert Duplication)

properties.text: Kenku Recall: Before making an ability check using a skill in which you have proficiency, you can expend one use of Kenku Recall to give yourself advantage on that check. You have a number of uses equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Mimicry: You can accurately mimic sounds you have heard, including voices. A creature that hears the sounds you make can tell they are imitations only with a successful Wisdom (Insight) check against DC 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Charisma modifier.

name: Kobold
description:
Kobolds are small reptilian humanoids with a connection to dragons. Though slight in stature, their ingenuity, tenacity, and draconic heritage make them formidable in numbers.

**Darkvision.** You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were in dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

**Draconic Cry.** As a bonus action, you let out a cry at your enemies within 10 feet of you. Until the start of your next turn, you and your allies have {rule:Advantage} on attack rolls against any of those enemies who could hear you. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

**Kobold Legacy.** Kobold's connections to dragons can manifest in unpredictable ways in an individual kobold. Choose one of the following legacy options:

- **Craftiness.** You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Arcana, Investigation, Medicine, Sleight of Hand, or Survival.
- **Defiance.** You have advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the {condition:Frightened} condition on yourself.
- **Draconic Sorcery.** You know one cantrip of your choice from the Sorcerer spell list. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for that cantrip (choose when you select this species).

properties.text: Draconic Cry: As a bonus action, cry at enemies within 10 feet. Until start of your next turn, you and your allies have advantage on attack rolls against those enemies who heard you. Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

options.label: Craftiness
options.description: You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Arcana, Investigation, Medicine, Sleight of Hand, or Survival.

options.label: Defiance
options.description: You have advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Frightened condition on yourself.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Frightened condition on yourself

options.label: Draconic Sorcery
options.description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the Sorcerer spell list. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it (choose when you select this legacy).

name: Lizardfolk
description:
Lizardfolk are cold-blooded reptilian humanoids with tough, scaly skin, powerful jaws, and a pragmatic, instinct-driven worldview. They are skilled swimmers and natural hunters.

**Bite.** You have a fanged maw that you can use to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with it, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier slashing damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

**Hold Breath.** You can hold your breath for up to 15 minutes at a time.

**Hungry Jaws.** You can throw yourself into a feeding frenzy. As a bonus action, you can make a special attack with your Bite. If the attack hits, it deals its normal damage, and you gain temporary hit points equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

**Natural Armor.** You have tough, scaly skin. When you aren't wearing armor, your base AC is 13 + your Dexterity modifier. You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield's benefits apply as normal while you use your natural armor.

**Nature's Intuition.** Thanks to your mystical connection to nature, you gain proficiency with two of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Stealth, or Survival.

**Speed.** Your walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

properties.text: Bite: Unarmed strike dealing 1d6 + Strength modifier slashing damage.
Hold Breath: Can hold breath for up to 15 minutes.
Hungry Jaws: As a bonus action, make a special Bite attack. On a hit, gain temporary HP equal to your Proficiency Bonus. Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

name: Minotaur
description:
Minotaurs are powerful, bull-headed humanoids known for their strength, ferocity, and innate sense of direction. They possess natural horn weapons and a supernatural bond to labyrinths.

**Horns.** You have horns that you can use to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier piercing damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

**Goring Rush.** Immediately after you take the {action:Dash} action on your turn and move at least 20 feet, you can make one melee attack with your Horns as a bonus action.

**Hammering Horns.** Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack as part of the {action:Attack} action on your turn, you can use a bonus action to attempt to push that target with your horns. The target must be within 5 feet of you and no more than one size larger than you. Unless it succeeds on a Strength saving throw against a DC equal to 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Strength modifier, you can push it up to 10 feet away from you.

**Labyrinthine Recall.** You always know which direction is north, and you have {rule:Advantage} on any Wisdom (Survival) check you make to navigate or track.

properties.text: Horns: Unarmed strike dealing 1d6 + Strength modifier piercing damage.
Goring Rush: After taking the Dash action and moving at least 20 feet, make one melee attack with your Horns as a bonus action.

Hammering Horns: After hitting with a melee attack as part of the Attack action, use a bonus action to attempt to push the target (within 5 ft, not more than one size larger). Target makes Strength save DC 8 + Proficiency Bonus + Strength modifier or is pushed up to 10 feet.
Labyrinthine Recall: Always know which direction is north.

name: Orc
description:
Orcs are a fearsome people, known for their physical might and tenacity. They typically have grayish or greenish skin, prominent lower canines, and powerful builds. Orc culture values strength and direct action, and many orcs adventure to prove their worth or follow a calling.

**Adrenaline Rush.** You can take the {action:Dash} action as a Bonus Action. When you do so, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Short Rest or Long Rest.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 120 feet.

**Relentless Endurance.** When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 Hit Point instead. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

properties.text: Adrenaline Rush: Bonus Action to Dash, gaining Proficiency Bonus Temporary HP. Usable Proficiency Bonus times per Short or Long Rest.
Relentless Endurance: Once per Long Rest, when reduced to 0 HP (not killed outright), drop to 1 HP instead.

name: Satyr
description:
Satyrs are fey creatures with the upper body of a humanoid and the lower body of a goat, complete with curved horns. They embody the spirit of revelry, music, and wild magic resistance.

**Creature Type.** You are a Fey.

**Ram.** You can use your head and horns to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier bludgeoning damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

**Magic Resistance.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws against spells.

**Mirthful Leaps.** Whenever you make a long jump or a high jump, you can roll a d8 and add the number rolled to the number of feet you cover, even when making a standing jump. This extra distance costs movement as usual.

**Reveler.** As an embodiment of revelry, you have proficiency in the Performance and Persuasion skills, and you have proficiency with one musical instrument of your choice.

properties.text: Ram: Unarmed strike dealing 1d6 + Strength modifier bludgeoning damage.
Mirthful Leaps: When making a long or high jump, roll a d8 and add the result to feet covered.

name: Sea Elf
description:
Sea Elves are aquatic elves native to the ocean depths, with webbed hands and feet and a natural affinity for the sea. They are counted as elves for all purposes and share the Trance ability of their surface kin.

**Child of the Sea.** You can breathe air and water, and you have {rule:Resistance} to cold damage.

**Darkvision.** You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

**Fey Ancestry.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} condition on yourself.

**Friend of the Sea.** Aquatic animals have an extraordinary affinity with your people. You can communicate simple ideas to any Beast that has a swimming speed. It can understand your words, though you have no special ability to understand it in return.

**Keen Senses.** You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

**Speed.** Your walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

**Trance.** You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness. Whenever you finish this trance, you can gain two proficiencies that you don't have, each one with a weapon or tool of your choice. You mystically acquire these proficiencies by drawing them from shared elven memory, and you retain them until you finish your next Long Rest.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Charmed condition on yourself

properties.text: Child of the Sea: Can breathe air and water.
Friend of the Sea: Can communicate simple ideas to any Beast with a swimming speed. No ability to understand it in return.

Trance: No need to sleep; immune to magical sleep. Long Rest takes only 4 hours in trance. After each trance, gain two temporary proficiencies (weapon or tool) drawn from elven memory; these last until the next Long Rest.

name: Shadar-Kai
description:
Shadar-kai are elves native to the Shadowfell, marked by its bleak power. Their skin and hair tend toward ashen gray tones, and they carry an air of somber fatalism tempered by stoic resilience.

**Blessing of the Raven Queen.** As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. Starting at 3rd level, you also gain resistance to all damage when you teleport using this trait. The resistance lasts until the start of your next turn. During that time, you appear ghostly and translucent.

**Darkvision.** You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

**Fey Ancestry.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} condition on yourself.

**Keen Senses.** You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

**Necrotic Resistance.** You have {rule:Resistance} to necrotic damage.

**Trance.** You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness. Whenever you finish this trance, you can gain two proficiencies that you don't have, each one with a weapon or tool of your choice. You mystically acquire these proficiencies by drawing them from shared elven memory, and you retain them until you finish your next Long Rest.

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Charmed condition on yourself

properties.text: Blessing of the Raven Queen: As a bonus action, teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest. At level 3+, gain resistance to all damage until start of your next turn when you use this teleport.

Trance: No need to sleep; immune to magical sleep. Long Rest takes only 4 hours in trance. After each trance, gain two temporary proficiencies (weapon or tool) drawn from elven memory; these last until the next Long Rest.

name: Shifter
description:
Shifters are humanoids with bestial features and a lycanthropic heritage. They can briefly tap the beast within, shifting into a more feral form to gain combat benefits.

**Bestial Instincts.** Channeling the beast within, you have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Acrobatics, Athletics, Intimidation, or Survival.

**Darkvision.** You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were in dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

**Shifting.** As a bonus action, you can assume a more bestial appearance. This transformation lasts for 1 minute, until you die, or until you revert to your normal appearance as a bonus action. When you shift, you gain temporary hit points equal to 2 × your Proficiency Bonus. You can shift a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Whenever you shift, you gain an additional benefit based on your Shifting form (chosen when you select this species):

- **Beasthide.** You gain 1d6 additional temporary hit points. While shifted, you have a +1 bonus to your Armor Class.
- **Longtooth.** When you shift and as a bonus action on your other turns while shifted, you can use your elongated fangs to make an unarmed strike dealing 1d6 + Strength modifier piercing damage.
- **Swiftstride.** While shifted, your walking speed increases by 10 feet. Additionally, you can move up to 10 feet as a reaction when a creature ends its turn within 5 feet of you. This reactive movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.
- **Wildhunt.** While shifted, you have advantage on Wisdom checks, and no creature within 30 feet of you can make an attack roll with advantage against you unless you're {condition:Incapacitated}.

properties.text: Shifting: As a bonus action, transform for 1 minute (or until dismissed as a bonus action or death). Gain temporary HP equal to 2 × Proficiency Bonus on shift. Proficiency Bonus times per Long Rest.

options.label: Beasthide
options.description: When you shift, gain 1d6 additional temporary HP. While shifted, gain +1 bonus to AC.

options.label: Longtooth
options.description: When you shift and as a bonus action on subsequent turns while shifted, make an unarmed strike with elongated fangs dealing 1d6 + Strength modifier piercing damage.

options.label: Swiftstride
options.description: While shifted, walking speed increases by 10 feet. As a reaction when a creature ends its turn within 5 feet of you, move up to 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

options.label: Wildhunt
options.description: While shifted, advantage on Wisdom checks, and no creature within 30 feet can make attack rolls with advantage against you unless you are Incapacitated.

name: Tabaxi
description:
Tabaxi are feline humanoids hailing from distant jungles, driven by an insatiable curiosity that leads them to travel the world in search of interesting stories, rare relics, and strange tales.

**Cat's Claws.** You can use your claws to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier slashing damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

**Cat's Talent.** You have proficiency in the Perception and Stealth skills.

**Darkvision.** You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

**Feline Agility.** Your reflexes and agility allow you to move with a burst of speed. When you move on your turn in combat, you can double your speed until the end of the turn. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you move 0 feet on one of your turns.

**Speed.** Your walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed.

properties.text: Cat's Claws: Unarmed strike dealing 1d6 + Strength modifier slashing damage.
Feline Agility: Once per combat sequence, double speed until end of turn. Can't use again until you move 0 feet on one of your turns.

name: Tiefling
description:
Tieflings are defined by an infernal heritage that traces back through their bloodline to a source of fiendish power. They are humanoid in most respects, but typically have horns, a tail, and eyes of a solid color. Despite their fearsome appearance, tieflings are no more inclined toward evil than any other people.

**Darkvision.** You have {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

**Fiendish Legacy.** You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a legacy from the options below. You gain the level 1 benefit of the chosen legacy. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn a higher-level spell as shown. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose when you select the legacy).

- **Abyssal.** You have {rule:Resistance} to Poison damage. You also know the {spell:Poison Spray} cantrip. At level 3: {spell:Ray of Sickness}. At level 5: {spell:Hold Person}.
- **Chthonic.** You have {rule:Resistance} to Necrotic damage. You also know the {spell:Chill Touch} cantrip. At level 3: {spell:False Life}. At level 5: {spell:Ray of Enfeeblement}.
- **Infernal.** You have {rule:Resistance} to Fire damage. You also know the {spell:Fire Bolt} cantrip. At level 3: {spell:Hellish Rebuke}. At level 5: {spell:Darkness}.

**Otherworldly Presence.** You know the {spell:Thaumaturgy} cantrip. When you cast it with this trait, the spell uses the same spellcasting ability you use for your Fiendish Legacy trait.

options.label: Abyssal
options.description: Resistance to Poison damage. Know Poison Spray cantrip. Also know Thaumaturgy. At level 3: Ray of Sickness (1/Long Rest). At level 5: Hold Person (1/Long Rest).

options.label: Chthonic
options.description: Resistance to Necrotic damage. Know Chill Touch cantrip. Also know Thaumaturgy. At level 3: False Life (1/Long Rest). At level 5: Ray of Enfeeblement (1/Long Rest).

options.label: Infernal
options.description: Resistance to Fire damage. Know Fire Bolt cantrip. Also know Thaumaturgy. At level 3: Hellish Rebuke (1/Long Rest). At level 5: Darkness (1/Long Rest).

name: Tortle
description:
Tortles are turtle-like humanoids with a tough shell that serves as natural armor. They are patient and methodical in temperament, with an instinctive connection to the natural world.

**Claws.** You have claws that you can use to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier slashing damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

**Hold Breath.** You can hold your breath for up to 1 hour.

**Natural Armor.** Your shell provides you a base AC of 17 (your Dexterity modifier doesn't affect this number). You can't wear light, medium, or heavy armor, but if you are using a shield, you can apply the shield's bonus as normal.

**Nature's Intuition.** Thanks to your mystical connection to nature, you gain proficiency with one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Stealth, or Survival.

**Shell Defense.** You can withdraw into your shell as an action. Until you emerge, you gain a +4 bonus to your AC, and you have advantage on Strength and Constitution saving throws. While in your shell, you are {condition:Prone}, your speed is 0 and can't increase, you have disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws, you can't take reactions, and the only action you can take is a bonus action to emerge from your shell.

modifiers.condition: natural shell armor (Dexterity modifier does not apply)

properties.text: Claws: Unarmed strike dealing 1d6 + Strength modifier slashing damage.
Hold Breath: Can hold breath for up to 1 hour.

Shell Defense: As an action, withdraw into shell. While withdrawn: +4 bonus to AC, advantage on Strength and Constitution saves, disadvantage on Dexterity saves, speed 0, can't take reactions. Only available action is a bonus action to emerge.

name: Triton
description:
Tritons are aquatic humanoids native to the ocean depths, self-appointed guardians of the deep who ventured to the surface world. They carry themselves with a noble bearing and a hint of arrogance born from long isolation.

**Amphibious.** You can breathe air and water.

**Control Air and Water.** You can cast {spell:Fog Cloud} with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast {spell:Gust of Wind} with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast {spell:Water Walk} with it. Once you cast any of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this species).

**Darkvision.** You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

**Emissary of the Sea.** You can communicate simple ideas to any Beast, Elemental, or Monstrosity that has a swimming speed. It can understand your words, though you have no special ability to understand it in return.

**Guardian of the Depths.** Adapted to the frigid ocean depths, you have {rule:Resistance} to cold damage.

**Speed.** Your walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

properties.text: Amphibious: Can breathe air and water.

Control Air and Water: Cast Fog Cloud (level 1+), Gust of Wind (level 3+), Water Walk (level 5+), each once per Long Rest. Can also use spell slots of appropriate level. Spellcasting ability is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose at creation).
Emissary of the Sea: Can communicate simple ideas to any Beast, Elemental, or Monstrosity with a swimming speed. No ability to understand it in return.

name: Yuan-Ti
description:
Yuan-ti are snake-like humanoids descended from humans who performed dark rituals to meld with serpents. They vary in appearance from nearly human to nearly serpentine, and are known for their cold intellect and resistance to magic and poison.

**Darkvision.** You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

**Magic Resistance.** You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws against spells.

**Poison Resilience.** You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {condition:Poisoned} condition on yourself. You also have {rule:Resistance} to poison damage.

**Serpentine Spellcasting.** You know the {spell:Poison Spray} cantrip. You can cast {spell:Animal Friendship} an unlimited number of times with this trait, but you can target only snakes with it. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast {spell:Suggestion} with this trait. Once you cast it, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of 2nd level or higher. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this species).

properties.condition: to avoid or end the Poisoned condition on yourself

properties.text: Animal Friendship (snakes only): Cast at will targeting only snakes. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability (choose at creation).
Suggestion (level 3+): Cast once per Long Rest; can also use 2nd-level or higher spell slots. Spellcasting ability is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma.
